question_text: A plane mirror produces an image that is:

options.label: A
options.text: Real, inverted, and the same size as the object

options.label: B
options.text: Virtual, upright, and the same size as the object

options.label: C
options.text: Virtual, inverted, and smaller than the object

options.label: D
options.text: Real, upright, and larger than the object

correct_answer: Virtual, upright, and the same size as the object
explanation: Plane mirrors always produce virtual images because the light rays appear to come from behind the mirror but don't actually meet there. These images are upright (the same way up as the object) and are the same size as the object. The image appears to be the same distance behind the mirror as the object is in front of it.
